children.heading: Establishment
children.label: a Establishment
children.text: The Secretary of the Interior shall establish within the Department of the Interior the National Geospatial Advisory Committee to provide advice and recommendations to the Chairperson of the Committee.

children.label: A
children.text: be appointed by the Chairperson of the Committee;

children.label: i
children.text: to generally achieve a balanced representation of the viewpoints of various interested parties involved in national geospatial activities and the development of the National Spatial Data Infrastructure; and

children.label: ii
children.text: with consideration of a geographic balance of residence of the members; and

children.label: i
children.text: States;

children.label: ii
children.text: local governments;

children.label: iii
children.text: regional governments;

children.label: iv
children.text: tribal governments;

children.label: v
children.text: private sector entities;

children.label: vi
children.text: geospatial information user industries;

children.label: vii
children.text: professional associations;

children.label: viii
children.text: scholarly associations;

children.label: ix
children.text: nonprofit organizations;

children.label: x
children.text: academia;

children.label: xi
children.text: licensed geospatial data acquisition professionals; and

children.label: xii
children.text: the Federal Government.

children.heading: Chairperson
children.label: 2 Chairperson
children.text: The Chairperson of the Committee shall appoint the Chairperson of the Advisory Committee.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Members shall be appointed for a term of 3 years, with the term of ⅓ of the members expiring each year.

children.heading: Vacancies
children.label: B Vacancies
children.text: Any vacancy in the Advisory Committee shall not affect its powers, but shall be filled in the same manner as the original appointment.

children.label: A
children.text: may not be appointed to more than 2 consecutive terms as a member of the Advisory Committee; and

children.label: B
children.text: after serving for 2 consecutive terms, is eligible to be appointed as a member of the Advisory Committee on and after the date that is 2 years after the end of the second consecutive term of the individual as a member of the Advisory Committee.

children.heading: Ethical requirements
children.label: 5 Ethical requirements
children.text: A member of the Advisory Committee may not participate in any specific-party matter (including a lease, license, permit, contract, claim, agreement, or related litigation) with the Department of the Interior in which the member has a direct financial interest.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: An individual serving on the day before , as a member of the National Geospatial Advisory Committee established by the Secretary of the Interior may serve as a member of the Advisory Committee until the end of the term of the individual under the appointment.October 5, 2018

children.heading: Limit on terms
children.label: B Limit on terms
children.text: Any period of service as a member of the National Geospatial Advisory Committee established by the Secretary of the Interior shall be considered a period of service as a member of the Advisory Committee for purposes of paragraph (4).
may be formed for the purposes of compiling information or conducting research;
shall be composed of members appointed by the Chairperson of the Advisory Committee;

shall act under the direction of the Chairperson of the Advisory Committee and the officer or employee designated under section 10(e) of the Federal Advisory Committee Act (5 U.S.C. App.) with respect to the Advisory Committee;1See References in Text note below.1
shall report the recommendations of the subcommittee to the Advisory Committee for consideration; and
shall meet as necessary to accomplish the objectives of the subcommittee, subject to the approval of the Chairperson of the Advisory Committee and the availability of resources.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Advisory Committee shall meet at the call of the Chairperson, not less than 1 time each year and not more than 4 times each year.

children.heading: Quorum
children.label: 2 Quorum
children.text: A majority of the members of the Advisory Committee shall constitute a quorum, but a lesser number of members may hold meetings or hearings.

children.heading: Duties of the Advisory Committee
children.label: e Duties of the Advisory Committee
A
children.text: the management of Federal and national geospatial programs;

children.label: B
children.text: the development of the National Spatial Data Infrastructure; and

children.label: C
children.text: implementation of this chapter;
review and comment on geospatial policy and management issues; and
ensure the views of representatives of non-Federal interested parties involved in national geospatial activities are conveyed to the Committee.

children.heading: Powers of the Advisory Committee
children.label: f Powers of the Advisory Committee

children.heading: Meetings
children.label: 1 Meetings
children.text: The Advisory Committee may hold meetings (which shall be open to the public) and sit and act at such times and places as the Advisory Committee considers advisable to carry out this chapter.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Advisory Committee, with the concurrence of the Chairperson of the Committee, may secure directly from any covered agency such information as the Advisory Committee considers necessary to carry out this chapter. Upon request of the Chairperson of the Advisory Committee, the head of such agency shall furnish such information to the Advisory Committee.

children.heading: Noncooperation
children.label: B Noncooperation
children.text: The Advisory Committee shall include in the comments of the Advisory Committee submitted under a discussion of any failure by a covered agency to furnish information in response to a request under subparagraph (A) of this paragraph.section 2802(c)(11) of this title

children.heading: Postal services
children.label: 3 Postal services
children.text: The Advisory Committee may use the United States mails in the same manner and under the same conditions as other agencies of the Federal Government.

children.heading: Non-Federal employees
children.label: A Non-Federal employees
children.text: A member of the Advisory Committee who is not an officer or employee of the Federal Government shall serve without compensation.

children.heading: Federal employees
children.label: B Federal employees
children.text: A member of the Advisory Committee who is an officer or employee of the Federal Government shall serve without compensation in addition to the compensation received for the services of the member as an officer or employee of the Federal Government.

children.heading: Travel expenses
children.label: 2 Travel expenses
children.text: The members of the Advisory Committee shall be allowed travel expenses, including per diem in lieu of subsistence, at rates authorized for employees of agencies under subchapter I of chapter 57 of title 5 while away from their homes or regular places of business in the performance of services for the Advisory Committee.

children.heading: Detail of Government employees
children.label: 3 Detail of Government employees
children.text: Any Federal Government employee may be detailed to the Committee to support the Advisory Committee without reimbursement, and such detail shall be without interruption or loss of civil service status or privilege.

children.heading: Staff support
children.label: 4 Staff support
children.text: The Office of the Secretariat established by the Committee under shall provide administrative support to the Advisory Committee.section 2802(d) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), the Federal Advisory Committee Act (5 U.S.C. App.) shall apply to the Advisory Committee.1

children.heading: No termination
children.label: 2 No termination
children.text: Section 14(a)(2) of the Federal Advisory Committee Act (5 U.S.C. App.) shall not apply to the Advisory Committee.1

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), the Advisory Committee shall terminate 10 years after .October 5, 2018

children.heading: Continuation
children.label: 2 Continuation
children.text: The Advisory Committee may be continued for successive 10-year periods by action taken by the Secretary of the Interior to renew the Advisory Committee before the date on which the Advisory Committee would otherwise terminate.
